question: Is the shoe upper material changed from breathable mesh to short-nap suede?
Does the new suede texture exhibit a visible directional pile or brush-stroke effect?
Are the shoelaces changed from fluorescent green to a slightly yellowed off-white color?
Is the original grey color tone of the shoe upper generally preserved?
Are all original stitching lines preserved in their correct positions and clearly defined?
Is the embossed 'STRIDE' logo text preserved, remaining sharp and legible?
Are the perforation positions on the shoe upper preserved without being filled or blurred?
Is the threading pattern and spatial arrangement of the laces unchanged?
Is the sole of the shoe unchanged in color and texture?
Is the overall shape and silhouette of the shoe identical to the original image?
Is the blurred track background (including lane lines and surface color) completely unchanged?
Is the original shallow depth of field (sharp shoe, blurred background) correctly maintained?
Is the image free from unnatural artifacts, such as color bleeding from the laces or patchy textures?
Is the lighting and shading on the new suede material consistent with the original scene?
